skill.name: setup-pre-commit
skill.description: Configure Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged, Prettier, type checking, and tests for a JavaScript or TypeScript repository.
skill.summary: Adds a standard Husky and lint-staged pre-commit workflow for project formatting, type checks, and tests.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command references and one weak-cryptography hit. The command references are true positives because the skill instructs agents to install packages, run npx, run project scripts, edit hook files, and commit changes; these are legitimate for this skill but require user review. The weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive, and I found no evidence of malware, data exfiltration, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Modifying External Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run Husky, lint-staged, typecheck, test, and commit-related commands in the user's repository. This is expected for pre-commit setup, but it can install dependencies, execute package scripts, and change repository behavior through Git hooks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit command examples and verification steps that run local project tooling. The commands align with the stated purpose, so the concern is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Automated Filesystem Changes to Project Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create or modify .husky/pre-commit, .lintstagedrc, .prettierrc, and package.json configuration. These changes are normal for the workflow, but they affect future commits and should be reviewed before publishing or committing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require creating and checking repository configuration files. This is legitimate setup behavior, but it changes developer workflow and may introduce unwanted hooks if applied without review.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, but the line is descriptive metadata about Husky, lint-staged, Prettier, type checking, and tests. No evidence of cryptographic code or insecure algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only the skill description and no cryptographic function names, keys, hashes, or encryption logic. The static high-risk result is not supported by the observed content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Tool Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks agents to run package-manager commands and project scripts such as Husky initialization, lint-staged, typecheck, and tests. These commands can execute local package lifecycle scripts or repository-defined scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are explicitly present and can execute local toolchains. Their use is expected for a setup skill, but the execution path depends on the user's repository.

content.value_statement: Manual pre-commit setup is easy to miss or configure inconsistently. This skill adds Husky, lint-staged, Prettier, type checking, and tests using a repeatable workflow.
actual_capabilities: Detects npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun from lockfiles and adapts commands.
Installs Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier as development dependencies.
Initializes Husky and creates a pre-commit hook for formatting, type checks, and tests.
Adds lint-staged configuration that runs Prettier on staged files.
Creates a default Prettier configuration only when one is missing.
Verifies expected hook files, package scripts, and lint-staged behavior.
limitations: It is intended for JavaScript and TypeScript repositories that use package.json.
It does not design custom linting, formatting, or test strategies.
It should not overwrite existing formatting configuration without review.
Repository-defined scripts can be slow or fail during commit verification.

use_cases.title: Standardize team commits
use_cases.description: Add consistent formatting and checks before commits in a shared JavaScript repository.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a new TypeScript project
use_cases.description: Create a basic pre-commit workflow before a project gains many contributors.

use_cases.title: Modernize existing project hygiene
use_cases.description: Introduce Husky and lint-staged to a repository that already has test or typecheck scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic setup
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants standard pre-commit hooks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged and Prettier in this repository.

prompt_templates.title: Respect existing config
prompt_templates.scenario: A repository may already have formatter settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add pre-commit hooks, but preserve any existing Prettier or lint-staged configuration unless a change is required.

prompt_templates.title: Include project checks
prompt_templates.scenario: A project already has scripts for validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure the pre-commit hook to run lint-staged first, then include existing typecheck and test scripts if they are present.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and apply safely
prompt_templates.scenario: A maintainer wants to understand all repository changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the current package manager and scripts, propose the Husky changes, then apply them and summarize every file changed.

output_examples.input: Set up pre-commit hooks in a pnpm TypeScript project.
output_examples.output: The assistant detects pnpm, installs the required dev dependencies, creates the Husky hook, configures lint-staged, and reports the files changed.

output_examples.input: Add formatting checks without changing my existing Prettier config.
output_examples.output: The assistant keeps the current Prettier configuration, adds lint-staged formatting for staged files, and avoids replacing existing formatter settings.

output_examples.input: Make commits run formatting, typecheck, and tests.
output_examples.output: The assistant adds a pre-commit hook that runs lint-staged before the repository's typecheck and test scripts, then verifies the setup.

best_practices: Review generated hook and configuration files before committing them.
Keep pre-commit checks fast enough for everyday development.
Confirm that typecheck and test scripts already work before adding them to the hook.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite existing team formatting rules without approval.
Do not add long integration tests to a pre-commit hook.
Do not run setup commands in an untrusted repository without reviewing package scripts.

faq.question: What package managers does this skill support?
faq.answer: It checks for npm, pnpm, yarn, and bun lockfiles, then adapts commands to the detected package manager.

faq.question: Does it always create a Prettier config?
faq.answer: No. It creates a default .prettierrc only when no Prettier configuration already exists.

faq.question: Will it add typecheck and test commands?
faq.answer: It adds them to the hook only when matching scripts exist in package.json.

faq.question: Can this change commit behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated Husky hook runs checks before commits, so failed checks can block a commit.

faq.question: Is this safe for monorepos?
faq.answer: It can help, but monorepos may need workspace-specific commands and hook placement review.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests or data exfiltration was found in the skill instructions.
